value:
Hi there, thanks for reaching out! To help you set up a default page in your Azure WebApp, you'll want to configure it through your web.config file or via the Azure portal settings. In web.config, you can specify default documents in the `<system.webServer>` section under `<defaultDocument>` — just make sure your HTML file is listed there. Alternatively, in the Azure portal, you can navigate to your WebApp's settings and look for the default documents configuration option.

If you've already added your HTML file via FTP and it's still not showing as the default page, double-check that the filename matches exactly what you've configured, and that there are no other files (like index.html or default.html) that might be taking priority. Let me know if you hit any snags with these steps and I'm happy to help troubleshoot further!
